incidents.event: At least 29 commercial ships including 13 tankers have been attacked or reported incidents since March 1 in the Gulf, Strait of Hormuz, or Gulf of Oman, with ongoing maritime security threats.
Several vessels reported being shadowed by Iranian fast-attack craft, causing temporary halt in traffic and anchoring in safe zones outside the strait.
Seizure of Epaminondas confirmed by Iranian authorities; crew held for investigative questioning after vessel attempted to evade IRGC patrols.
Seizure of MSC Francesca confirmed by Iranian authorities amid escalating maritime tensions; vessel detained for alleged violations.
U.S. Navy seized an Iranian oil tanker en route to China in the Indian Ocean as part of ongoing enforcement operations amid rising tensions.
Iranian IRGC Navy seized the container ship Epaminondas after firing warning shots; vessel sustained heavy damage but crew remained safe.
Iranian IRGC Navy seized the container ship MSC Francesca for allegedly lacking transit permits; vessel moved into Iranian waters.
The container ship Euphoria was fired upon by Iranian forces but escaped damage and continued transit towards Gulf of Oman.
A container ship was struck near Oman causing heavy damage to the bridge.
An outbound cargo ship west of Iran was fired upon and stopped in the water; no injuries reported.
